description: Acquisition manifest for real-app ground truth. Each family declares how its references are captured on this machine. Tiers: live-web (agent-driven browser capture), native-local (installed macOS app, pixels only), online-only (official docs/press/video scrape), manual-inbox (human-provided screenshots filed via tools/import-reference.mjs). Additional no-auth methods: app-store-api (iTunes lookup screenshotUrls — real current-version UI), yt-dlp+ffmpeg (frame-mining official vendor videos). Recipes + acquisition ladder: docs/reference-and-asset-sourcing.md; loop design: docs/fidelity-loop-plan-2026-07-05.md.

targets.label: web-app-chat
targets.notes: Primary ground truth. Desktop app is Electron over the same UI; agents cannot drive the desktop app, so desktop-only chrome (title bar, traffic lights) arrives via manual-inbox.

targets.label: mac-app
targets.notes: Validated 2026-07-13: /Applications/Claude.app is installed and signed in. osascript System Events gets front-window {position,size}, then screencapture -x -R<bounds> captures real retina pixels without clicking anything. The desktop app is now the PRIMARY Claude ground truth per owner direction: Home page (chat+cowork merged into one composer with a Chat|Cowork pill toggle) + Code page (Claude Code desktop, sessions grouped by repo) behind a Home|Code sidebar toggle. Owner also drops manual screenshots (manual-inbox). Captures show the owner's real account/sessions - screenshot pixels stay local/gitignored; measured-tokens.json + notes are the shareable ground truth. Codex (good computer-use) can drive the app for additional states/pages in future passes.

fallbacks: codex computer-use: delegate app-state navigation + screenshots to Codex when a page can't be captured without UI driving
manual-inbox: user screenshots of the desktop app (chat/cowork/code pages)
reference/claude/frame-study/ launch-video extracts

docs-press: support.claude.com Help Center articles ship official illustration crops of real components (e.g. articles/8887527-customizing-your-appearance-settings 'Color mode' card shows the composer in light/dark) -- no-auth, useful for chrome-color grounding when claude-in-chrome isn't connected, but these are small staged crops, not full-page captures

targets.label: web-app
targets.notes: Requires user's OpenAI session. Codex desktop app not driveable; manual-inbox for desktop states.

fallbacks: reference/codex/app-screenshots/ (12 shots, light/dark)

targets.label: desktop-app-editor
targets.notes: Validated 2026-07-13: /Applications/Figma.app installed + logged in. open -a Figma, osascript System Events front-window bounds, screencapture -x -R<bounds>. Opens on the last file; shows the real UI3 chrome: dark tab strip w/ traffic lights + file tabs (the Mac window chrome), left icon rail (File/Agents/Assets/Tools/Variables), light side panels, dark canvas. Owner's real file names visible - pixels stay local/gitignored.

targets.label: editor
targets.notes: Open a scratch design file in the user's account; never a client file.

fallbacks: reference/figma/actual-app/source-index.json (Help Center scrape, refreshable via online-only pass)

targets.label: finder-window
targets.notes: Fixed window size before capture; pixels only, no style extraction.

fallbacks: reference/open-source/macos-web donor repo (pinned)

targets.label: chrome-window
targets.notes: Tab strip, omnibox, window chrome. Page content is ours by design.

targets.label: app-store-screens
targets.notes: curl 'https://itunes.apple.com/lookup?bundleId=com.microsoft.Word&country=us' -> results[0].screenshotUrls (validated 2026-07-09). Edit the mzstatic size suffix upward (800x500bb.jpg -> 2400x1500bb.png) for full res. Real current Word-for-Mac UI; content is lightly staged — trust chrome/geometry/colors, discount document content.

targets.label: web-app
targets.notes: Word for the web is free with a personal Microsoft account. Canvas, typography, comment balloons, dialogs = ground truth; web ribbon and window chrome differ from Mac desktop — record that caveat in the manifest note. Flip tier to live-web once a Microsoft session exists in Chrome.

targets.label: docs-scrape
targets.notes: Official docs/marketing screenshots; staged, critic must discount.

fallbacks: video-frame-mine: Microsoft 365 YouTube channel Word-for-Mac tutorials (yt-dlp + ffmpeg, recipe in docs/reference-and-asset-sourcing.md)
reference/word/
reference/open-source/fluentui (pinned)

targets.label: app-store-screens
targets.notes: Re-validated 2026-07-13: still v16.110.3 (currentVersionReleaseDate 2026-07-07), same 6 screenshotUrls, upsize to 2400x1500bb.png for full res. Local pixels restored for all 3 originally-described shots (app-store-mac-grid-formula-bar, app-store-mac-sheet-tabs-status-bar, app-store-mac-chart-contextual-ribbon; reference/excel/2026-07-09/, content unchanged from 2026-07-09 filing) plus a 4th NEW shot filed to reference/excel/2026-07-13/app-store-mac-titlebar-comments-pane (screenshotUrls[4] 'Collaborate in real time') -- the only App Store shot showing the full title bar (traffic lights, AutoSave, editable doc title, Saved indicator, Search box) and the Comments pane + Review-tab comment controls. 2 shots remain unfiled: screenshotUrls[0] 'Get insights on your data' (tilted 3D hero, low value) and screenshotUrls[5] 'Professionally curated templates' (File > New template-gallery picker, moderate value -- candidate for a future pass if that surface state is needed).

targets.label: web-app
targets.notes: Excel for the web: grid, formula bar, cell styling = ground truth; ribbon/chrome differ from Mac desktop. Retried 2026-07-13: a Chrome browser IS now connected via claude-in-chrome (list_connected_browsers returned one device), but navigating to excel.office.com redirects straight to login.microsoftonline.com with no existing session (confirmed via screenshot -- blank sign-in form, no account pre-filled). Per hard rule (never log in to anything), this rung stays blocked until a free personal Microsoft account is already signed into the connected browser session; filing that pre-authenticated session is the only way to flip this to live-web without an agent performing a login.

fallbacks: video-frame-mine: Microsoft 365 YouTube Excel-for-Mac tutorials
reference/open-source/fluentui (pinned)

targets.label: app-store-screens
targets.notes: Same iTunes-lookup recipe as word.

targets.label: web-app
targets.notes: PowerPoint for the web: slide canvas, thumbnail rail idiom = ground truth; ribbon/chrome differ from Mac desktop.

fallbacks: video-frame-mine: Microsoft 365 YouTube PowerPoint-for-Mac tutorials
reference/powerpoint/
reference/open-source/fluentui (pinned)

targets.label: video-frame-mine
targets.notes: Adobe's official video channel: 1080p-4K tutorials showing the real Premiere UI at rest — the best real-pixel source for this family (not on the Mac App Store, app not installed here). Pick frames with no overlays/zooms; file with --source '<video url> @ <timestamp>'. Recipe: docs/reference-and-asset-sourcing.md Part 1.4.

targets.label: docs-scrape
targets.notes: Adobe help + launch-video frame studies. App not installed here.

fallbacks: press: news.adobe.com press images / Adobe brand kit
manual-inbox: 7-day Premiere trial screenshots if the user opts in

yt-dlp+ffmpeg on Adobe Video's periodic 'What's New in Premiere | <Year> Updates' uploads (@AdobeVideo) — short (~4min), title-carded with the exact version number (e.g. '26.0'), and cycles through several panels at rest (Effect Controls/Motion, Timeline, Lumetri Color > Curves, in-app Adobe Stock panel) with few overlays; best single video to re-mine each refresh. If yt-dlp isn't on PATH and `brew install yt-dlp` fails to link (e.g. an unrelated keg permission error), `python3 -m pip install --user --break-system-packages yt-dlp` works standalone and needs no linking.

targets.label: app-chrome-default
targets.notes: Validated 2026-07-13: /Applications/Slack.app is installed and already signed into the user's own workspace (v4.50.143). Fixed window to 1440x900 (AppleScript position/size), screencapture -R of the window bounds, filed via tools/import-reference.mjs --image. Captures purple sidebar + icon rail, workspace switcher, channel list, top search bar, message list with reactions/threads, open thread panel, rich-text compose box. This is a live, real, private workspace (actual names/messages) -- discount all text/avatar content, trust chrome geometry/colors/typography/iconography only; screenshot.png is gitignored (reference/*/20*/**/screenshot*.png), manifest.json is not. No login was performed or attempted -- the app was already authenticated when this was discovered. CAUTION: clicking generic accessibility matches (e.g. 'button whose description contains Close') inside the live message list can hit an in-message hyperlink and open the default browser instead of the intended app control (happened once this session, landed on Slack's public web sign-in page harmlessly -- closed the tab without interacting). Prefer precise UI-element targeting or leave transient panels (e.g. an open thread) as-is rather than risk another stray click.

targets.label: app-store-screens
targets.notes: Validated 2026-07-13: v4.50.143, 9 screenshotUrls, upsize to 2400x1500bb.png for full res. All 9 filed to reference/slack/actual-app/ (source-index.json) covering the purple sidebar chrome + icon rail, channel/DM message list with attachments and reactions, thread panel, live huddle video grid + control bar, Canvas doc, Slack Connect 'add people' modal, Lists table, Slackbot AI side panel, Apps directory grid, and the Workflow Builder Reminder-step modal. Real current-version UI composited into marketing cards; trust chrome geometry/colors/typography, discount the staged copy in headlines and avatar photos. Kept as a cross-check even now that native-local is available -- higher-res and covers surfaces (huddle grid, Canvas, modals) the default desktop view doesn't show.

targets.label: web-app
targets.notes: Slack web client is the same UI as desktop (Electron wraps the web app). Superseded as primary ground truth by the native-local desktop app now that it's installed and signed in; still useful for cross-checking web-only chrome (browser tab/omnibox) if ever needed.

targets.label: docs-scrape
targets.notes: Help Center articles ship real annotated UI diagrams (e.g. the sidebar-anatomy callout image filed alongside the App Store set); media-kit is brand assets (logo/wordmark), not app-chrome screenshots.

fallbacks: video-frame-mine: Slack's official YouTube channel (@SlackHQ) product-tour and launch videos
reference/slack/actual-app/source-index.json (App Store + Help Center scrape, refreshable via online-only pass)
manual-inbox: user screenshots of their own logged-in Slack workspace if they opt in
